description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched roof with reddish-brown tiles and a prominent overhang, providing ample shade to the wraparound porch supported by slender columns. The structure is single-storied, with a rectangular footprint and a combination of open and enclosed spaces. The porch area is clearly defined, with wooden flooring and open sides, while the main building is enclosed with light-colored walls. The roof design includes intersecting hips and ridges, adding architectural interest and depth. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with some grouped on the side and a large opening visible on one facade. The overall style is reminiscent of a Ranch or Modern bungalow, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide, reddish-brown tiled hip roof, wraparound porch, and light-colored walls, featuring asymmetrical window placement and a spacious, shaded veranda.
description_view_0: Front and side of the bungalow showing the wraparound porch, supporting columns, and the main entrance area beneath the extended roof.
description_view_1: Rear and partial side view highlighting the roof's intersecting hips and a large window opening on the back wall.
description_view_2: Opposite side and partial front view, displaying the porch's depth, grouped side windows, and the extended roof overhang.
description_view_3: Direct front view emphasizing the full width of the porch, the supporting columns, and the symmetrical roofline.
